sources.title: Physicists Rule Out the Production of Dangerous Black Holes at the LHC
sources.author: Phys.org (reporting Giddings & Mangano)
sources.role: the core safety argument: any micro black hole evaporates; cosmic rays already test it

sources.title: LHC Black Holes for the Terminally Un-Reassured
sources.author: 4 gravitons (working physicist blog)
sources.role: a physicist walking a skeptic through why the safety case holds, assumption by assumption

sources.title: Review of the Safety of LHC Collisions (abstract)
sources.author: Ellis, Giddings, Mangano, Tkachev, Wiedemann (LSAG, 2008)
sources.role: the formal peer-reviewed safety review — the authoritative no-risk conclusion

sources.title: A Lawyer's View of the Risk of Black Hole Catastrophe at the LHC
sources.author: Phys.org (reporting Eric E. Johnson)
sources.role: the critical view: questions whether the safety assurances are as airtight as claimed

sources.title: Rossler-Cook versus Hawking and the CERN Detectors
sources.author: Lifeboat Foundation blog
sources.role: the fear case in its own words (Rössler's growth argument) — later judged a misunderstanding of GR

sources.title: Safety of high-energy particle collision experiments
sources.author: Wikipedia
sources.role: overview mapping every hypothesized risk (black holes, strangelets, vacuum decay) and its rebuttal

sources.title: Collider court case finally closed
sources.author: NBC News
sources.role: narrative of the Wagner/Sancho lawsuit — how the fear played out legally and was dismissed

claims.text: If the LHC could produce a dangerous stable object, cosmic rays striking the Earth would already have done so

attestations.verbatim_span: such events, if they could produce dangerous, stable black holes, would have already done so
attestations.context: logical conclusion from the cosmic-ray argument

attestations.verbatim_span: If the LHC could destroy the world, cosmic rays would have already done so.
attestations.context: the author's 'best but most unsatisfying' argument
attestations.framing: author calls this the best argument while noting it does not explain why

attestations.verbatim_span: anything dangerous that the LHC could create would already have been produced by cosmic rays long ago
attestations.context: CERN's shared cosmic-ray argument across all disaster scenarios

claims.text: A black hole small enough for the LHC to produce would evaporate by Hawking radiation almost instantly, too fast to leave the machine or reach the Earth's centre

attestations.verbatim_span: A microscopic black hole would thus be very unstable, disappearing in the tiniest fraction of a second.
attestations.context: consequence of Hawking evaporation

attestations.verbatim_span: a black hole small enough that the LHC could produce it would radiate away to nothing in almost an instant: not long enough to leave the machine, let alone fall to the center of the Earth
attestations.context: the Hawking-radiation argument

attestations.verbatim_span: Any microscopic black holes produced at the LHC are expected to decay by Hawking radiation before they reach the detector walls.
attestations.context: the Hawking-radiation argument

claims.text: The survival of dense astronomical bodies bombarded by such collisions for billions of years shows the collisions cannot be dangerous

attestations.verbatim_span: Nature has been performing this type of experiment for billions of years, not only on our planet and sun, but also on much denser objects, like neutron stars and white dwarfs. Their enormous age reassures us that physics doesn't exhibit the bizarre behavior that LHC opponents have suggested.
attestations.context: Giddings's core empirical argument

attestations.verbatim_span: The stability of astronomical bodies indicates that such collisions cannot be dangerous.
attestations.context: core empirical argument

attestations.verbatim_span: have been bombarded with cosmic particles of significantly higher energies than that of RHIC and other man made colliders for billions of years, without any harm to the Solar System
attestations.context: the astrophysical safety argument for RHIC

claims.text: Cosmic rays with energies comparable to or higher than the LHC's strike the Earth's atmosphere constantly

attestations.verbatim_span: collisions with the same power as those to be produced at the LHC—in the trillion electron volt range—happen all the time on Earth, when cosmic rays from deep space (protons with extremely high energies) collide with molecules in the atmosphere
attestations.context: the cosmic-ray safety argument

attestations.verbatim_span: High-energy particles called cosmic rays enter the atmosphere every day, some of which are at energies comparable to the LHC.
attestations.context: the cosmic-ray argument

attestations.verbatim_span: including ultra-high-energy cosmic rays observed to impact Earth with energies far higher than those in any man-made collider.
attestations.context: the cosmic-ray comparison

claims.text: The LHC will not destroy the Earth

attestations.verbatim_span: Would we be doomed? The short answer is no.
attestations.context: Giddings and Mangano's bottom line

attestations.verbatim_span:
Could the LHC have killed us all?
No, no it could not.
attestations.context: the blog's opening answer

claims.text: The plaintiffs assert the LHC could create dangerous micro black holes or strangelets that grow or release radiation and could destroy the Earth

attestations.verbatim_span: the LHC experiments have the potential to create low velocity micro black holes that could grow in mass or release dangerous radiation leading to doomsday scenarios
attestations.context: the opponents' core mechanism
attestations.framing: risk-side claim

attestations.verbatim_span: the LHC could create globe-gobbling black holes, strangelets or other exotic and catastrophic phenomena
attestations.context: the plaintiffs' underlying claim

claims.text: The LHC could produce a tiny, extremely short-lived and harmless black hole

attestations.verbatim_span: the LHC could produce a tiny, extremely short-lived (read: harmless) black hole
attestations.context: framing the one black-hole outcome physicists consider plausible

claims.text: Physicists discount the possibility of a stable black hole that could grow dangerous

attestations.verbatim_span: they discount the possibility of a stable black hole -- one with the chance to grow into something worth worrying about -- as much more science fiction than science
attestations.context: summary of the physics consensus
attestations.framing: 'science fiction than science' dismisses the stable-BH scenario

claims.text: Giddings and Mangano examined known astrophysical phenomena to determine the likelihood the LHC produces stable black holes

attestations.verbatim_span: examining known astrophysical phenomena, using what scientists already know about the universe to determine the likelihood that the LHC will produce stable black holes on Earth
attestations.context: describing the study's method

claims.text: Scientists almost universally agree that black holes evaporate by emitting Hawking radiation

attestations.verbatim_span: scientists almost universally agree that black holes evaporate, emitting particles called Hawking radiation
attestations.context: first argument against stable black holes

claims.text: A charged black hole from cosmic rays would be drained of energy by Earth's material before travelling far

attestations.verbatim_span: a charged black hole produced by cosmic rays wouldn't be able to travel very far before it was drained of energy by the material of the Earth
attestations.context: charged-black-hole sub-case

claims.text: The existence of humanity proves any charged black holes produced in the atmosphere are safe

attestations.verbatim_span: If charged black holes have ever been produced in our atmosphere, our existence is proof they are safe.
attestations.context: anthropic form of the cosmic-ray argument

claims.text: An LHC black hole would likely be charged because it would come from collisions between charged quark pairs

attestations.verbatim_span: At the LHC, a black hole would likely be charged because it would have been produced by collisions between quark pairs (quarks have charge).
attestations.context: why the charged case is the relevant one for the LHC

claims.text: Neutralization and Hawking radiation are linked quantum processes, so a neutral non-radiating black hole is nearly impossible

attestations.verbatim_span: Neutralization and Hawking radiation are intricately linked quantum processes; if one is occurring, the other should be, too.
attestations.context: why the neutral stable case is almost ruled out a priori

claims.text: A slow-growing neutral stable black hole would take longer than the sun's lifetime to grow, so the sun would burn out before any danger

attestations.verbatim_span: its growth would take place on a time scale longer than the natural life of our sun. That means our star would burn out before we were in danger.
attestations.context: first sub-case of neutral stable black holes

claims.text: Fast-growing neutral stable black holes are excluded because white dwarfs and neutron stars would be destroyed by them yet still exist

attestations.verbatim_span: much denser objects than Earth and the sun, such as white dwarfs and neutron stars, which are already close to collapsing in on themselves, would be destroyed by such black holes. The simple fact that these stars still exist excludes this second case.
attestations.context: second sub-case, ruled out by stellar survival

claims.text: The overwhelming majority of the scientific community agrees the stable-black-hole scenarios are not credible

attestations.verbatim_span: The overwhelming majority of the scientific community agrees that the stable scenarios are crazy anyway.
attestations.context: Giddings's closing appeal to consensus

claims.text: Some physicists hoped to make a black hole at the LHC because it would be evidence for large curled-up extra dimensions

attestations.verbatim_span: it would have been evidence for extra dimensions, curled-up dimensions much larger than the tiny ones required by string theory
attestations.context: noting black-hole production was a hoped-for result, not only a feared one

claims.text: The LHC speeds particles to high but not unprecedentedly high energies

attestations.verbatim_span: The LHC speeds up particles to high energies, but not unprecedentedly high energies.
attestations.context: setup for the cosmic-ray argument

claims.text: The cosmic-ray argument may not hold for future higher-energy colliders that cosmic rays do not commonly match

attestations.verbatim_span: we could build a collider with enough energy that cosmic rays don’t commonly meet it
attestations.context: author flagging a limit of the cosmic-ray argument
attestations.framing: concession that weakens the flagship no-risk argument for future machines

claims.text: Hawking radiation has never been measured and is only a theoretical expectation

attestations.verbatim_span: never measured Hawking radiation
attestations.context: author's caveat about the Hawking-radiation argument
attestations.framing: concession that the second no-risk argument rests on unconfirmed theory

claims.text: A black hole's gravitational pull is no stronger than any other object of the same mass at the same distance

attestations.verbatim_span: due to gravity, no stronger than the gravity of any other object with the same mass at the same distance
attestations.context: correcting the vacuum-cleaner misconception

claims.text: Absorbing an entire cylinder of matter straight through the Earth would add almost no mass to the tiny black hole

attestations.verbatim_span: Absorbing an entire cylinder through the Earth makes barely any difference.
attestations.context: first worst-case accretion estimate

claims.text: An object dropped through the Earth takes about 84.5 minutes to traverse it, independent of its mass

attestations.verbatim_span: the time the object takes to travel through the Earth is independent of its mass, and equal to roughly 84.5 minutes
attestations.context: setting up the oscillation calculation

claims.text: An oscillating LHC black hole would take about ten to the sixty-seven years to double its mass, while the universe is only about ten to the ten years old

attestations.verbatim_span: Ten to the sixty-seven years . Our universe is only about ten to the ten years old.
attestations.context: the worst-case doubling-time result

claims.text: The Sun will enter its red giant phase and swallow the Earth long before such a black hole could grow dangerous

attestations.verbatim_span: the Sun will enter its red giant phase, and swallow the Earth
attestations.context: comparison of timescales showing the sun ends Earth first

claims.text: Even the most pessimistic version of the calculation shows the black hole is not dangerous

attestations.verbatim_span: the most pessimistic way to walk through the calculation
attestations.context: author's closing verdict on the worst-case estimate

claims.text: The 2003 LHC Safety Study Group concluded LHC collisions presented no danger

attestations.verbatim_span: The safety of collisions at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) was studied in 2003 by the LHC Safety Study Group, who concluded that they presented no danger.
attestations.context: the prior safety review this paper updates

claims.text: New experimental and theoretical results confirm, update, and extend the earlier no-danger conclusion

attestations.verbatim_span: enable us to confirm, update and extend the conclusions of the LHC Safety Study Group
attestations.context: the review's overall finding

claims.text: The LHC reproduces collisions at centre-of-mass energies below those of cosmic rays that have bombarded Earth for billions of years

attestations.verbatim_span: The LHC reproduces in the laboratory, under controlled conditions, collisions at centre-of-mass energies less than those reached in the atmosphere by some of the cosmic rays that have been bombarding the Earth for billions of years.
attestations.context: the cosmic-ray comparison

claims.text: The review studied vacuum bubbles, magnetic monopoles, microscopic black holes, and strangelets and found no associated risks

attestations.verbatim_span: we study the possible production at the LHC of hypothetical objects such as vacuum bubbles, magnetic monopoles, microscopic black holes and strangelets, and find no associated risks
attestations.context: scope of the hypothetical-object analysis

claims.text: If some microscopic black holes were stable, cosmic-ray-produced ones would be stopped inside the Earth or other astronomical bodies

attestations.verbatim_span: If some microscopic black holes were stable, those produced by cosmic rays would be stopped inside the Earth or other astronomical bodies.
attestations.context: the stable-black-hole sub-case

claims.text: The stability of astronomical bodies strongly constrains any accretion rate, so microscopic black holes present no conceivable danger

attestations.verbatim_span: The stability of astronomical bodies constrains strongly the possible rate of accretion by any such microscopic black holes, so that they present no conceivable danger.
attestations.context: quantitative bound on accretion

claims.text: RHIC particle-production measurements match thermodynamic models, severely constraining strangelet production at the LHC, which presents no danger

attestations.verbatim_span: the good agreement of measurements of particle production at RHIC with simple thermodynamic models constrains severely the production of strangelets in heavy-ion collisions at the LHC, which also present no danger
attestations.context: the strangelet sub-case

claims.text: Peer-reviewed studies have shown there is no significant risk of an LHC doomsday scenario

attestations.verbatim_span: peer-reviewed studies have shown that there is no significant risk of an LHC doomsday scenario
attestations.context: reporter stating the physics consensus

claims.text: The continued existence of the Earth is living evidence of the safety of these scenarios

attestations.verbatim_span: The fact that the Earth still exists is living evidence of the safety of these scenarios.
attestations.context: anthropic form of the cosmic-ray argument

claims.text: In 1999 physicists found the forces of modern accelerators were insufficient to create a black hole, at least in a four-dimensional world

attestations.verbatim_span: the forces created by modern accelerators were insufficient to create a black hole - at least in a four-dimensional world.
attestations.context: early RHIC-era analysis

claims.text: Black holes could be produced if extra dimensions exist, as some theories allow

attestations.verbatim_span: black holes could be produced if there were extra dimensions, a possibility in some theories.
attestations.context: the extra-dimensions caveat that reopened the question

claims.text: CERN found the LHC would likely produce black holes but they would rapidly evaporate via Hawking radiation

attestations.verbatim_span: the LHC would likely produce black holes, but that they would rapidly evaporate due to Hawking radiation.
attestations.context: CERN's reexamination after the extra-dimensions finding

claims.text: Rossler calculated LHC-produced black holes might grow fast enough to end the world slightly more than five years after the first full-energy collisions

attestations.verbatim_span: LHC-produced black holes might grow fast enough that the world might end slightly more than five years after the LHC’s first full-energy collisions.
attestations.context: Johnson highlighting Rossler as a critic of the Hawking-radiation orthodoxy
attestations.framing: risk-side claim, attributed to a named outside critic

probability_estimates.value: world ends ~5 years after first full-energy collisions

claims.text: In 2008 Mangano and Giddings turned to the cosmic-ray argument rather than the Hawking-radiation argument, which was becoming less persuasive

attestations.verbatim_span: turned to the cosmic ray argument rather than the Hawking radiation argument, which was becoming less persuasive.
attestations.context: how the LSAG safety case shifted its main argument
attestations.framing: concedes the Hawking-radiation argument had weakened

claims.text: Cosmic-ray black holes could slip through the Earth, while LHC black holes could remain nearby and slowly gain mass

attestations.verbatim_span: black holes produced by cosmic rays could potentially slip through the Earth, which is made mostly of empty space, while black holes produced by the LHC could remain in the vicinity for a long time, slowly gaining mass.
attestations.context: the disanalogy that forced the white-dwarf argument
attestations.framing: identifies why the cosmic-ray argument alone is insufficient

claims.text: A kind of white dwarf, eight of which have been observed, could hold black holes for a long time, so their survival is evidence the LHC is safe

attestations.verbatim_span: a kind of white dwarf star, eight of which have been observed, could likely hold black holes for a long time, and so their continued existence must serve as living evidence that the LHC is safe.
attestations.context: the white-dwarf survival argument

claims.text: CERN's Scientific Policy Committee announced to the public that the results excluded any possibility of risk, going beyond Giddings and Mangano's 'no significance' conclusion

attestations.verbatim_span: took an extra step, announcing to the public that the results excluded
attestations.context: Johnson noting an overstatement from 'no significance' to 'any possibility'
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that CERN publicly overstated the certainty of the result

claims.text: If black holes were smaller than CERN calculated they could conceivably sail through white dwarfs as well as the Earth, so the white-dwarf argument is not conclusive

attestations.verbatim_span: they could conceivably sail through white dwarfs as well as the Earth
attestations.context: Plaga's unpublished critique of the white-dwarf argument
attestations.framing: risk-side critique that the load-bearing white-dwarf argument has a hole

claims.text: Most of the arguments made in favour of the collider lack robustness

attestations.verbatim_span: most of the arguments you hear in favor of the collider lack robustness.
attestations.context: Johnson's overall assessment of the public safety case
attestations.framing: risk-leaning meta-claim about argument quality, not about danger itself

claims.text: Physicists quoted in the media on LHC safety seem not to have engaged the literature in any depth

attestations.verbatim_span: Many of the physicists quoted in the media on LHC safety issues seem not to have engaged with the literature in any depth
attestations.context: Johnson's critique of public communication

claims.text: Using the Hand formula, the risk of the LHC destroying the world can be computed as either infinite or nothing by tweaking the inputs slightly

attestations.verbatim_span: the risk of the LHC destroying the world can be calculated to be either infinite or nothing simply by tweaking the inputs just a tiny bit
attestations.context: Johnson on the instability of cost-benefit analysis for this case
attestations.framing: methodological critique of quantitative risk analysis

claims.text: In 2003 the two papers addressing RHIC strangelet-disaster concerns were found to contain conceptual math errors affecting their conclusions

attestations.verbatim_span: the two papers written to address safety concerns about a potential strangelet disaster at the RHIC both contained conceptual math errors that affected their conclusions.
attestations.context: Johnson citing a precedent for safety-paper error
attestations.framing: risk-side evidence that safety analyses have been wrong before

claims.text: A small observational error in the eight white dwarfs could undermine the safety argument, since the conclusion rests on those objects

attestations.verbatim_span: Even a relatively small observational error of the eight white dwarfs could undermine the safety argument
attestations.context: Johnson on the fragility of the load-bearing white-dwarf evidence
attestations.framing: identifies the single load-bearing piece of the safety case

claims.text: Giddings and Mangano conclude no conceivable risk, but LHC risk is not zero because the assessment must include the possibility they are mistaken

attestations.verbatim_span: But it does not follow that LHC risk is zero: An accurate assessment of risk must include the possibility that Giddings and Mangano themselves are mistaken.
attestations.context: Johnson's central epistemic point
attestations.framing: the key model-uncertainty argument on the risk side

claims.text: Halting the LHC would destroy billions of dollars of investment and many physicists' careers, so individual physicists are better off focusing on research than on safety and legal issues

attestations.verbatim_span: an individual physicist is better off concentrating on the science research than getting involved in safety and legal issues
attestations.context: Johnson's account of physicists' incentives to avoid the safety debate
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that incentives bias physicists against scrutinizing safety

claims.text: CERN's group culture tends to push dissenters to the fringe and may artificially inflate the certainty of unanimously held views

attestations.verbatim_span: such a group culture also has the tendency to push dissenters to the fringe, and perhaps artificially inflate the certainty of unanimously enforced views
attestations.context: Johnson's sociological critique of the safety consensus
attestations.framing: risk-side claim casting the safety consensus as partly sociological

claims.text: Scientists have a responsibility to the rest of the world to ensure their experiments are safe

attestations.verbatim_span: Scientists have a responsibility to the rest of the world to ensure that their experiments are safe.
attestations.context: Johnson's normative premise

claims.text: Courts have the power to perform an in-depth analysis, reviewing evidence and gathering testimony from scientists with no personal stake in the LHC

attestations.verbatim_span: the courts have the power to perform an in-depth analysis - involving a thorough review of the evidence and gathering testimony from scientists with no personal stake in the LHC
attestations.context: Johnson's proposed remedy: judicial review

claims.text: Whether the LHC represents an intolerable danger is an open question

attestations.verbatim_span: whether the LHC represents an intolerable danger is, in my view, an open question
attestations.context: Johnson's explicit bottom line
attestations.framing: the risk-side flagship is hedged as 'open question', not an assertion of danger

claims.text: Johnson did not attempt a definitive answer on the danger but argues the courts should

attestations.verbatim_span: I have not endeavored to provide a definitive answer. But I think the courts should.
attestations.context: Johnson declining to adjudicate the physics himself

claims.text: Courts must develop tools to handle such scientific complexity or risk undoing the rule of law

attestations.verbatim_span: Courts must develop tools to deal meaningfully with such complexity.
attestations.context: Johnson's closing warning

claims.text: Hawking radiation is dead since the Telemach result and its precursors appeared, and no one has defended it

attestations.verbatim_span: Hawking radiation is dead ever since the Telemach result and its precursors surfaced on the web.
attestations.context: Rossler's foundational claim undercutting the evaporation argument
attestations.framing: risk-side claim asserting the main safety mechanism is refuted

claims.text: The 454 trillion collisions produced in the LHC's proton runs are evidence of its safety

attestations.verbatim_span: I submit the 454 000 000 000 000 collisions produced by the LHC in their proton runs as evidence of its safety.
attestations.context: Bill Johnson's empirical safety argument
attestations.framing: no-risk claim from the accumulated collision record

claims.text: Since a world-ending black hole would already be irreversible, continued argument must assume none has been created, leaving 454 trillion examples of the LHC's safety

attestations.verbatim_span: Thus your continued writing on this topic must assume that a MBH has not yet been created in which case there are 454 000 000 000 000 examples of the safety of the LHC.
attestations.context: Bill Johnson's logical rebuttal to Rossler's delay defence
attestations.framing: no-risk argument turning the delay claim against itself

claims.text: There is a delay of several years before a dangerous black hole becomes evident as imminent

attestations.verbatim_span: there is a delay of several years before the ultra-atrocious becomes evident as being imminent.
attestations.context: Rossler's prediction that danger is delayed
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that immunizes the theory against the empirical collision record

claims.text: The probability that the irreversible event has already occurred is a fixed number, taken as 2 percent for lack of a better estimate

attestations.verbatim_span: the probability of this irreversible event having already occurred is a fixed number — take 2 percent for lack of a better estimate.
attestations.context: Rossler quantifying the per-run catastrophe probability
attestations.framing: risk-side quantified estimate

claims.text: Every black hole has the same chance to get stuck inside the Earth and automatically cause exponential cancerous growth inside

attestations.verbatim_span: every black hole has the same chance to get stuck inside earth to automatically cause the exponential cancerous growth inside.
attestations.context: Rossler's accretion mechanism
attestations.framing: risk mechanism asserted as automatic

claims.text: We do not know the collision energy needed for the creation of micro black holes

attestations.verbatim_span: We do not know the collision energy needed for the creation of micro black holes.
attestations.context: Tottoli listing unknowns
attestations.framing: risk-side uncertainty claim

claims.text: There are important differences between most natural collisions and collisions at the LHC

attestations.verbatim_span: there are important differences between most of the natural collisions and collisions at the LHC.
attestations.context: Tottoli challenging the cosmic-ray equivalence
attestations.framing: risk-side disanalogy claim

claims.text: The problem arises only when there is a refusal to check an undisproved risk

attestations.verbatim_span: refusing to check an undisproved risk
attestations.context: Rossler framing the dispute as a refusal to investigate
attestations.framing: risk-side framing of the safety debate

claims.text: Holding the safety conference cannot be the consequence of a judicial decision but is a matter of political discretion for the defendant

attestations.verbatim_span: sie kann aber nicht Folge einer gerichtlichen Entscheidung sein.
attestations.context: the Cologne court ruling on whether a conference could be ordered
attestations.framing: establishes the court did not order a safety conference

claims.text: There was no independent safety assessment nor a quantitative calculation of the probability of the risk

attestations.verbatim_span: there was no independent safety assessment nor a quantitative calculation (for the probability) of the risk.
attestations.context: Tottoli arguing an independent, quantitative safety assessment is still missing
attestations.framing: risk-side methodological critique of the existing safety reviews

claims.text: Not all known risks are considered in CERN's LSAG safety report

attestations.verbatim_span: Not all known risks are considered in the LSAG safety report of CERN.
attestations.context: Tottoli's recurring incompleteness claim
attestations.framing: risk-side claim

claims.text: There is an 80-page critical assessment of the Giddings-Mangano paper

attestations.verbatim_span: there is a critical assessment with 80 pages of the GM paper too
attestations.context: Tottoli pointing to an existing critique for review

claims.text: The defendant in the Cologne case was the Federal Republic of Germany, since the court had no jurisdiction over CERN directly

attestations.verbatim_span: the defendant in this case was the Federal Republic of Germany.
attestations.context: correcting who the defendant was

claims.text: The court did not request a safety conference

attestations.verbatim_span: the court did not request anything.
attestations.context: TRMG's reading of the ruling after receiving the record
attestations.framing: settles the recurring dispute: opinion, not order

claims.text: In the court's opinion the defendant had fulfilled all necessary obligations with regard to risk assessment

attestations.verbatim_span: the defendant has fulfilled all necessary obligations with regard to risk assessment
attestations.context: the court's finding on the adequacy of the risk assessment

claims.text: Not all risks have been addressed by CERN, and no treatise checks whether there is any sign of a dangerous threshold

attestations.verbatim_span: there is no treatise which looks whether there is any sign of a dangerous threshold
attestations.context: Tottoli arguing the safety reviews are incomplete
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that the safety reviews left gaps

claims.text: Not all risks have been addressed by CERN

attestations.verbatim_span: not all risks have been addressed by CERN.
attestations.context: Tottoli restating the incompleteness claim
attestations.framing: risk-side claim

claims.text: Every rationally conceivable risk has already been exhaustively addressed

attestations.verbatim_span: I think every rationally conceivable risk has been exhaustively addressed already.
attestations.context: TRMG rebutting the incompleteness claim
attestations.framing: no-risk claim that scrutiny is already complete

claims.text: The German science ministry concluded that the intended research at the LHC establishes no danger potential

attestations.verbatim_span: Forschungen am LHC kein Gefahrenpotential begründen
attestations.context: German BMBF answer to a parliamentary question, declining to convene a safety conference
attestations.framing: official government no-risk conclusion

claims.text: Producing Higgs particles at the LHC would not imply any risk

attestations.verbatim_span: How would this imply that producing Higgs particles at the LHC involves any risk whatsoever?
attestations.context: PassingByAgain challenging Tottoli to specify a mechanism
attestations.framing: no-risk rebuttal posed as a rhetorical question

claims.text: It could be dangerous to experiment near an artificial Big Bang at extreme luminosity and possibly produce unnaturally slow Higgs particles

attestations.verbatim_span: it could be eventually dangerous to experiment near an artificial Big Bang, with a such extreme luminosity and possibly produce Higgs particles which could be unnaturally slow
attestations.context: Tottoli's speculative Higgs danger
attestations.framing: risk-side speculation, hedged as a question

claims.text: Cosmic rays at such energies mostly collide with stationary particles of celestial bodies, unlike head-on collider collisions

attestations.verbatim_span: comic rays with such energies mostly collide with stationary particles of celestial bodies
attestations.context: Tottoli invoking the fixed-target versus collider disanalogy
attestations.framing: risk-side challenge to the cosmic-ray safety argument

claims.text: Head-on proton-proton and lead-lead collisions at LHC energies with both particles moving relative to astronomical bodies are very rare in the universe and perhaps never occur near the surfaces of celestial bodies

attestations.verbatim_span: Head-on proton-proton and lead-lead collisions with both particles having almost an equal velocity relative to astronomical bodies with energies as at the LHC (mostly the ones with higher energies) are very rare in the universe and perhaps happen never or very seldom near (50cm or less) the surface of celestial bodies.
attestations.context: Tottoli's collider-versus-fixed-target disanalogy
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that the cosmic-ray analogy is not exact for head-on collisions

claims.text: The kind of high-energy cosmic rays is not known but only assumed

attestations.verbatim_span: The sort/kind of these high energy cosmic rays is also not known but only assumed.
attestations.context: Tottoli questioning knowledge of cosmic-ray composition
attestations.framing: risk-side uncertainty claim

claims.text: At CERN, pairs of protons or lead ions collide very frequently and very near the surface of dense materials

attestations.verbatim_span: At CERN we know that pairs of protons or lead ions collide very frequently and very near the surface of dense materials.
attestations.context: Tottoli contrasting collider geometry with cosmic-ray collisions

claims.text: CERN and its safety groups behave mostly as denying all risks instead of searching for risks, which could lead to danger

attestations.verbatim_span: behave mostly as denying all risks, instead of searching for risks
attestations.context: Tottoli's meta-critique of CERN's safety posture
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that the safety process is adversarial rather than exploratory

claims.text: The community should actively search for not-yet-considered risks

attestations.verbatim_span: we should actively search for not yet considered risks.
attestations.context: Tottoli's proposed norm

claims.text: Inflation involves the Higgs field as a whole, so there is no conceivable mechanism by which producing Higgs bosons at the LHC could trigger it

attestations.verbatim_span: inflation is a phenomenon that involves the Higgs field as a whole, there is no conceivable mechanism by which the production of Higgs bosons at the LHC can trigger it.
attestations.context: PassingByAgain's decisive rebuttal of the Higgs-inflation risk
attestations.framing: no-risk claim grounded in the field/particle distinction

claims.text: The energy-level transition responsible for inflation has already happened and the Higgs field is now in a low-energy state

attestations.verbatim_span: the transition between different energy levels that is responsible for inflation *has already happened*, and the Higgs field is now in a low-energy state
attestations.context: why a new inflation cannot be triggered now

claims.text: To claim a risk one must describe a physical mechanism through which it could materialize

attestations.verbatim_span: You must be able to describe a mechanism through which the risk you are talking about could materialize.
attestations.context: PassingByAgain placing the burden of mechanism on the claimant
attestations.framing: methodological standard for a credible risk claim

claims.text: Official court documents make clear that Rossler overstated how supportive the Cologne court was of his position

attestations.verbatim_span: it is clear that you have overstated how supportive the cologne court was of your position.
attestations.context: Bill Johnson on Rossler's characterization of the ruling

claims.text: Walter Wagner eventually gave up his US federal lawsuit

attestations.verbatim_span: eventually gave up his similar US Federal lawsuit.
attestations.context: Penner noting the fate of the parallel US case

claims.text: Rossler calculated that Earth's habitability persists only 50 months after the capture of a molecular-mass black hole

attestations.verbatim_span: the habitability of the Earth persists only 50 months past the point of the capture of a black hole of molecular mass
attestations.context: a critic (rpenner) restating Rossler's long-standing assertion to question where it was published
attestations.framing: presented by the critic as an unpublished, unverifiable claim

claims.text: Rossler's theorem underpinning the risk claim was never written up or reviewed by leading mathematicians

attestations.verbatim_span: Is it not unfair to proclaim that no-one has found flaws in a purported theorem which was never written up and widely read by the world’s best mathematicians?
attestations.context: critic questioning the evidentiary status of Rossler's theorem
attestations.framing: rhetorical question implying the theorem lacks scrutiny

claims.text: The anti-LHC risk claim is comparable to a speculative hypothesis that microchips will cause radiation poisoning, lacking any evidentiary basis

attestations.verbatim_span: the entirely speculative hypothesis that one day microchips will cause people to fall over due to self-radiation poisoning
attestations.context: critic's analogy dismissing the anti-LHC argument as unfalsifiable speculation
attestations.framing: hostile analogy equating the risk claim with baseless speculation

claims.text: None of the anti-LHC activists presented a persuasive case

attestations.verbatim_span: None of the anti-LHC activists have seemed persuasive to me.
attestations.context: critic's overall assessment

claims.text: In 2008 anti-LHC activists raised alarm and the world press amplified it

attestations.verbatim_span: In 2008, the anti-LHC activists shouted “Fire” and the world press heard and repeated them.
attestations.context: critic characterizing the 2008 media episode
attestations.framing: loaded 'shouting Fire' framing casting the activists as causing unwarranted panic

claims.text: Natural cosmic-ray collisions differ from LHC collisions, and the kind of high-energy particles involved is not known

attestations.verbatim_span: there are various differences between natural collisions and the ones at the LHC and for example the kind of high energy particles is not known
attestations.context: Tottoli challenging the cosmic-ray safety analogy
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that the cosmic-ray analogy may not hold

claims.text: An inflation-like mechanism might cause danger when something unnatural is done in small-scale experiments

attestations.verbatim_span: How can you be sure whether the mechanism of inflation (which is believed to be observed on large scales in the universe) can not cause a danger if something unnatural is done by experiments on small scales?
attestations.context: Tottoli raising a speculative danger mechanism as a rhetorical question
attestations.framing: hedged as a question rather than an assertion

claims.text: Hawking's silence on the risk argument may simply reflect that he has more important things to do

attestations.verbatim_span: One possible reason Hawking is silent on this matter is that he has better things to do.
attestations.context: critic explaining Hawking's non-engagement

claims.text: No proof or safety argument has been given that creating local oscillations of the Higgs field by experiments is absolutely safe

attestations.verbatim_span: creating local oscillations of the Higgs field by experiments is absolutely safe
attestations.context: Tottoli demanding a safety proof for Higgs-field disturbances
attestations.framing: risk-side demand for a positive safety proof

claims.text: The Big Rip might be triggered by high-energy collisions or disturbances of the Higgs field

attestations.verbatim_span: could it be triggered by some sort of high energy collisions or disturbances of the Higgs field?
attestations.context: Tottoli speculating about a Big Rip trigger
attestations.framing: risk-side speculation, hedged as a question

claims.text: If LHC collisions could trigger a Higgs vacuum transition, cosmic-ray collisions would trigger it as well

attestations.verbatim_span: If the LHC collisions could trigger such a transition, cosmic-ray collisions would trigger it as well.
attestations.context: applying the cosmic-ray argument to the Higgs vacuum-transition scenario

claims.text: For a Higgs vacuum transition nothing needs to stop in matter, so LHC and cosmic-ray collisions are equivalent

attestations.verbatim_span: Nothing needs to stop in matter in this scenario, thus LHC collisions and cosmic-ray collisions are equivalent.
attestations.context: why the cosmic-ray argument is airtight for the Higgs case
attestations.framing: distinguishes the Higgs argument as free of the black-hole accretion loophole

claims.text: The complications of the cosmic-ray argument for stable black holes all concern whether the black holes stop in matter and start accreting

attestations.verbatim_span: all the complications of the cosmic-ray argument for hypothetical stable black holes are related to the question of whether such black holes will be stopped in matter and start accreting.
attestations.context: naming the load-bearing complication of the black-hole safety case
attestations.framing: identifies exactly where the black-hole cosmic-ray argument needs patching

claims.text: The Higgs's properties are well understood, so claiming danger requires devising a physical mechanism by which collisions could trigger a Higgs-field transition

attestations.verbatim_span: the properties and interactions of the Higgs are pretty well understood. You cannot just wave your hands and claim a danger, you must first devise a physical mechanism by which the collisions could trigger a transition of the Higgs field.
attestations.context: PassingByAgain placing the burden of mechanism on the risk claimant
attestations.framing: methodological point: a bare possibility is not a risk without a mechanism

claims.text: The LSAG has not provided a safety argument against fanciful risks such as LHC-generated dragons, illustrating the futility of demanding disproof of every conceivable risk

attestations.verbatim_span: the LSAG has not provided a safety argument against that risk either
attestations.context: the 'dragons' reductio against demanding disproof of arbitrary risks
attestations.framing: rhetorical reductio of the unfalsifiable-risk demand

claims.text: The 2003 independent review concluded that, like the RHIC, LHC particle collisions pose no conceivable threat

attestations.verbatim_span: they concluded that, like current particle experiments such as the RHIC, the LHC particle collisions pose no conceivable threat.
attestations.context: the first CERN-mandated safety review

claims.text: The 2008 review, by CERN-affiliated physicists not involved in the LHC experiments, reaffirmed the safety of LHC collisions using research since 2003

attestations.verbatim_span: prepared by a group of physicists affiliated to CERN but not involved in the LHC experiments, reaffirmed the safety of the LHC collisions in light of further research conducted since the 2003 assessment.
attestations.context: the second safety review (LSAG)

claims.text: The 2008 report was reviewed and endorsed by a CERN committee of 20 external scientists and by the APS Division of Particles & Fields

attestations.verbatim_span: It was reviewed and endorsed by a CERN committee of 20 external scientists and by the executive committee of the Division of Particles & Fields of the American Physical Society
attestations.context: independent endorsement of the safety report

claims.text: The physical conditions and collision events at the LHC, RHIC and other experiments occur naturally and routinely in the universe without hazardous consequences

attestations.verbatim_span: the physical conditions and collision events which exist in the LHC, RHIC and other experiments occur naturally and routinely in the universe without hazardous consequences
attestations.context: the report's basis for ruling out doomsday

claims.text: RHIC studies concluded beyond reasonable doubt that heavy-ion experiments there will not endanger the planet

attestations.verbatim_span: beyond reasonable doubt, heavy-ion experiments at RHIC will not endanger our planet
attestations.context: quoted conclusion of RHIC safety studies

claims.text: There is powerful empirical evidence against the possibility of dangerous strangelet production

attestations.verbatim_span: powerful empirical evidence against the possibility of dangerous strangelet production.
attestations.context: quoted conclusion on strangelets

claims.text: Frank Close likened the chance of strangelet creation to winning the lottery three weeks in succession, noting people wrongly believe that is possible

attestations.verbatim_span: the chance of this happening is like you winning the major prize on the lottery 3 weeks in succession; the problem is that people believe it is possible to win the lottery 3 weeks in succession.
attestations.context: Frank Close characterizing the strangelet risk

claims.text: Physicists cannot demonstrate zero probability of catastrophic events and can only calculate an upper limit for the likelihood

attestations.verbatim_span: Physicists are unable to demonstrate experimental and astrophysical constraints of zero probability of catastrophic events
attestations.context: the critics' demand that risk be excluded, and its epistemic limit
attestations.framing: risk-side epistemic point: safety can only bound, not zero out, the probability

claims.text: Martin Rees calculated an upper limit of 1 in 50 million for the probability that RHIC would produce a global catastrophe or black hole

attestations.verbatim_span: Martin Rees had calculated an upper limit of 1 in 50 million for the probability that RHIC will produce a global catastrophe or black hole
attestations.context: Rees's published upper bound

claims.text: Rees reported he is not losing sleep over the collider and trusts the scientists who built it

attestations.verbatim_span: trusts the scientists who have built it.
attestations.context: Rees distancing himself from catastrophist readings of his number

claims.text: Richard Posner, Toby Ord and others have argued the LHC experiments are too risky to undertake

attestations.verbatim_span: have argued that the LHC experiments are too risky to undertake.
attestations.context: named proponents of the precautionary position
attestations.framing: risk-side conclusion from non-physicist risk scholars

claims.text: The Marburger committee left open the possibility that relativistic cosmic-ray impact products might behave differently while transiting Earth than at-rest RHIC products

attestations.verbatim_span: relativistic cosmic ray impact products might behave differently while transiting earth
attestations.context: a caveat the RHIC safety report did not close
attestations.framing: risk-side residual: the cosmic-ray analogy has an unclosed gap

claims.text: Claims that black holes were created at RHIC rest only on a mathematical AdS/CFT correspondence, not the same physical phenomena

attestations.verbatim_span: it is similar mathematically. Therefore, RHIC collisions might be described by mathematics relevant to theories of quantum gravity within AdS/CFT, but the described physical phenomena are not the same.
attestations.context: correcting a BBC report that RHIC made black holes

claims.text: The LHC team received death threats and threatening emails and phone calls demanding the experiment be halted

attestations.verbatim_span: they had received death threats and threatening emails and phone calls demanding the experiment be halted.
attestations.context: public reaction to the risk claims

claims.text: Brian Greene answered that a black hole produced under Geneva would not swallow Switzerland or devour the Earth

attestations.verbatim_span: It's a reasonable question with a definite answer: no.
attestations.context: Greene reassuring New York Times readers

claims.text: A 16-year-old girl in India committed suicide after becoming distressed about doomsday predictions on a news channel covering the LHC

attestations.verbatim_span: committed suicide, having become distressed about predictions of an impending
attestations.context: human cost of the doomsday coverage

claims.text: Walter Wagner claimed the chance of the LHC destroying the Earth was 50%, reasoning it will either happen or it won't

attestations.verbatim_span: he believed the chance of the LHC destroying the Earth to be 50%, since it will either happen or it won't.
attestations.context: Wagner on The Daily Show
attestations.framing: an explicit probabilistic fallacy from the lead opponent

claims.text: The Standard Model predicts LHC energies are far too low to create black holes

attestations.verbatim_span: LHC energies are far too low to create black holes
attestations.context: the default no-black-hole expectation

claims.text: If extra spatial dimensions exist, it would be possible to create micro black holes at the LHC at about one per second

attestations.verbatim_span: it would be possible to create micro black holes at the LHC at a rate of the order of one per second.
attestations.context: the extra-dimensions scenario

claims.text: By standard calculations any such micro black holes are harmless because they would quickly decay by Hawking radiation

attestations.verbatim_span: these are harmless because they would quickly decay by Hawking radiation
attestations.context: the Hawking-decay argument

claims.text: There is broad consensus on the reality of Hawking radiation, but no experiment has had the sensitivity to find direct evidence for it

attestations.verbatim_span: there is broad consensus among physicists on the reality of Hawking radiation, but so far no experiment has had the sensitivity required to find direct evidence for it.
attestations.context: LSAG caveat on the Hawking-radiation argument
attestations.framing: concedes the key mechanism is unconfirmed by experiment

claims.text: Even a stable micro black hole would be unable to accrete matter dangerously because of its Planck-length size

attestations.verbatim_span: even if micro black holes were produced by the LHC and were stable, they would be unable to accrete matter in a manner dangerous for the Earth due to their very small size, on the order of the Planck length.
attestations.context: LSAG accretion argument

claims.text: The continued existence of dense bodies like neutron stars and white dwarfs, as well as the Earth, rules out the LHC producing any dangerous black holes

attestations.verbatim_span: The continued existence of such dense bodies, as well as the Earth, rules out the possibility of the LHC producing any dangerous black holes.
attestations.context: LSAG stable-black-hole argument (block quote)

claims.text: If strangelets exist and were produced at the LHC, they could initiate a runaway process converting all the planet's nuclei to strange matter

attestations.verbatim_span: initiate a runaway fusion process in which all the nuclei in the planet would be converted to strange matter
attestations.context: the strangelet disaster mechanism
attestations.framing: risk mechanism, conditional on strangelets existing

claims.text: The probability of strangelet creation decreases at higher energies, so the LHC is less likely to produce them than its predecessors

attestations.verbatim_span: The probability of the creation of strangelets decreases at higher energies.
attestations.context: why higher LHC energy reduces strangelet risk

claims.text: Thermodynamics makes forming a cold strangelet condensate about as likely as producing an ice cube in a furnace

attestations.verbatim_span: it is about as likely as producing an icecube in a furnace.
attestations.context: second-law argument against strangelet formation

claims.text: Otto Rossler argues micro black holes created in the LHC could grow exponentially

attestations.verbatim_span: argues that micro black holes created in the LHC could grow exponentially.
attestations.context: Rossler's central risk claim
attestations.framing: risk-side claim that did not pass peer review

claims.text: Nicolai reviewed Rossler's paper and concluded it would not pass the referee process in a serious journal

attestations.verbatim_span: this text would not pass the referee process in a serious journal.
attestations.context: expert rebuttal of Rossler

claims.text: Giulini and Nicolai concluded Rossler's argument concerns only general relativity, makes no logical connection to LHC physics, and is neither valid nor self-consistent

attestations.verbatim_span: his argument concerns only the General Theory of Relativity (GRT), and makes no logical connection to LHC physics; the argument is not valid; the argument is not self-consistent.
attestations.context: detailed rebuttal of Rossler

claims.text: Plaga concluded LHC safety studies had not definitely ruled out a catastrophic threat from microscopic black holes, including possible danger from Hawking radiation

attestations.verbatim_span: LHC safety studies have not definitely ruled out the potential catastrophic threat from microscopic black holes, including the possible danger from Hawking radiation emitted by black holes.
attestations.context: Plaga's arXiv critique
attestations.framing: risk-side critique of the safety case

claims.text: Casadio, Fabi, and Harms published a paper ruling out the catastrophic growth of black holes in the scenario Plaga considered

attestations.verbatim_span: ruling out the catastrophic growth of black holes in the scenario considered by Plaga.
attestations.context: rebuttal of Plaga

claims.text: Plaga's paper has not been published in a peer-reviewed journal

attestations.verbatim_span: has not been published in a peer-reviewed journal.
attestations.context: status of the risk-side critique

claims.text: The LSAG reaffirmed and extended the conclusion that LHC collisions present no danger and there are no reasons for concern

attestations.verbatim_span: LHC collisions present no danger and that there are no reasons for concern
attestations.context: the 2008 LSAG update

claims.text: The APS Division of Particles and Fields stated the report explains why there is nothing to fear from particles created at the LHC

attestations.verbatim_span: this report explains why there is nothing to fear from particles created at the LHC
attestations.context: DPF endorsement

claims.text: A 2009 Physics Letters B paper summarizes proofs aimed at ruling out any possible black hole disaster at the LHC

attestations.verbatim_span: summarizes proofs aimed at ruling out any possible black hole disaster at the LHC
attestations.context: the Koch-Bleicher-Stocker exclusion paper

claims.text: Wagner and Sancho filed a complaint against CERN and its American collaborators seeking to halt the LHC

attestations.verbatim_span: filed by Walter L. Wagner and Luis Sancho against CERN and its American collaborators
attestations.context: the US lawsuit

claims.text: The plaintiffs demanded a permanent injunction until the LHC could be demonstrated reasonably safe within industry standards

attestations.verbatim_span: a permanent injunction until the LHC can be demonstrated to be reasonably safe within industry standards.
attestations.context: the relief the plaintiffs sought

claims.text: The LSAG review found no basis for any concerns about the consequences of new particles or forms of matter the LHC could produce

attestations.verbatim_span: no basis for any concerns about the consequences of new particles or forms of matter that could possibly be produced by the LHC
attestations.context: the LSAG report's bottom line

claims.text: The US Government called the plaintiffs' claimed hazards overly speculative and not credible

attestations.verbatim_span: overly speculative and not credible
attestations.context: the government's dismissal motion

claims.text: The Hawaii court dismissed the suit on the grounds that it had no jurisdiction over the LHC project

attestations.verbatim_span: granting the motion to dismiss on the grounds that it had no jurisdiction over the LHC project.
attestations.context: basis of the US dismissal
attestations.framing: dismissed on jurisdiction, not on the science

claims.text: A suit filed in the European Court of Human Rights alleged the LHC posed grave risks to the 27 EU member states and was summarily rejected the same day

attestations.verbatim_span: alleged that the Large Hadron Collider posed grave risks for the safety of the 27 member states of the European Union and their citizens.
attestations.context: Rossler-led European suit

claims.text: Johnson argued it is not clear that any particle-physics testimony should be allowed in the courtroom

attestations.verbatim_span: Given such a state, it is not clear that any particle-physics testimony should be allowed in the courtroom
attestations.context: Johnson's expert-testimony dilemma

claims.text: Only long-trained particle physicists can understand the risk arguments, but that very investment makes them highly biased in favour of the experiments

attestations.verbatim_span: any such persons, by reason of this huge personal investment, will inevitably be highly biased in favor of the experiments
attestations.context: the conflict-of-interest core of Johnson's dilemma
attestations.framing: risk-side epistemic point about who is competent to judge safety

claims.text: The German Constitutional Court rejected the injunction petition as unfounded, stating the opponents had failed to produce plausible evidence for their theories

attestations.verbatim_span: stating that the opponents had failed to produce plausible evidence for their theories.
attestations.context: the German court ruling

claims.text: A federal appeals court shut down a challenge to the LHC that was based on claims it could destroy the world

attestations.verbatim_span: last week a federal appeals court shut down a challenge to Europe's Large Hadron Collider that was based on claims it could destroy the world.
attestations.context: the ruling being reported

claims.text: A trio of physicists including Wilson, Glashow, and Wilczek filed a brief that the suggested doomsday scenarios had no realistic chance of occurring

attestations.verbatim_span: a friend-of-the-court brief pointing out that the doomsday scenarios suggested by Wagner and Sancho had no realistic chance of occurring
attestations.context: eminent physicists backing the no-risk position in court

claims.text: The three-judge panel rejected the appeal for legal rather than scientific reasons

attestations.verbatim_span: a three-judge panel rejected the appeal for legal rather than scientific reasons.
attestations.context: clarifying the basis of the ruling
attestations.framing: the legal closure did not adjudicate the science

claims.text: Speculative fear of future harm does not constitute an injury in fact sufficient to confer legal standing

attestations.verbatim_span: Speculative fear of future harm does not constitute an injury in fact sufficient to confer standing.
attestations.context: the court's standing analysis

claims.text: Even a decision in Wagner's favour would have no impact on CERN or collider operations and would not give him relief

attestations.verbatim_span: such a decision would have no impact on CERN or Collider operations, and would not afford Wagner the relief he seeks.
attestations.context: the court's redressability analysis

claims.text: A similar court case in Germany was thrown out because the challenger could not give a coherent account of how her fears would come about

attestations.verbatim_span: give a coherent account of how her fears would come about
attestations.context: a parallel dismissal in Germany

claims.text: Wagner intends to seek review of the panel's ruling by the entire Ninth Circuit, so the case may not be completely closed

attestations.verbatim_span: he intends to seek a review of the three-judge panel's ruling by the entire Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals.
attestations.context: update noting the litigation may continue
